event: Holy Family with a Garland of Flowers
symbols: flower garland framing — creation's beauty surrounding the Creator made flesh
description: A painting of the Holy Family surrounded by an elaborate garland of flowers. The central scene shows the Madonna and Child with Joseph, framed by a richly detailed floral wreath painted by Jan Brueghel the Elder in his characteristic botanical precision. The flowers include roses, tulips, and other blooms.
theological_description: The floral garland surrounding the Holy Family represents creation paying homage to its Creator. As the Psalmist declares: 'The heavens declare the glory of God, and the sky above proclaims his handiwork' (PSA.19.1). The flowers recall the Song of Solomon's garden imagery (SNG.2.1-2), and Jesus's own words: 'Consider the lilies of the field, how they grow... even Solomon in all his glory was not arrayed like one of these' (MAT.6.28-29).

scripture_refs.reason: Creation declaring God's glory, the flowers as creation's homage
Garden and flower imagery of the Song of Solomon
